assumptions: The question concerns IFRS consolidated financial statements.
The issue is the accounting model for foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable that has been recognised.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting as a relevant top-level model and includes highly probable forecast transactions, including in some cases forecast intragroup transactions, as possible hedged items.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting for recognised assets or liabilities as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model relevant where a receivable has been recognised.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRIC 16 guidance on hedges of net investments in foreign operations, which is a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
